name: Brute Force
summary: Try every pair of lines and keep the largest area — O(n²).

lineExplanations.3: Get n, the number of vertical lines.
lineExplanations.4: Track the maximum area found so far.
lineExplanations.5: Outer loop: choose the left line i.
lineExplanations.6: Inner loop: choose a right line j to the right of i.
lineExplanations.7: Water height is limited by the shorter of lines i and j.
lineExplanations.8: Width is the distance between i and j.
lineExplanations.9: Area = height × width for this pair.
lineExplanations.10: Keep the larger of the current best and this area.
lineExplanations.11: Return the largest area found over all pairs.

syntaxExplanations.2: def declares maxArea; self is the object, height is the input list.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(height) counts the lines; stored in n.
syntaxExplanations.4: Assigns mx = 0, the running best area.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n) loops i over 0 … n-1.
syntaxExplanations.6: Nested loop: j starts at i+1 so each pair is tried once.
syntaxExplanations.7: min(a, b) returns the shorter of the two heights.
syntaxExplanations.8: Subtraction gives the width j - i.
syntaxExplanations.9: * multiplies height by width to get the area.
syntaxExplanations.10: max(a, b) keeps the larger of mx and area.
syntaxExplanations.11: return hands back the best area.
